body.title: Score Igor CP1-6 against approach-frame; propose CP6 reframe for Akien review
body.result: CP1-6 scored. 5 already approach-framed. CP6 mixed-shape flagged, 5 reframe options proposed (A-E); Igor's direct call: keep original — hard edge is load-bearing axiom, not pessimism. Rationale saved to project_cp6_hard_edge.md. Metric updated: rules_reviewed += 6, rules_kept_with_rationale += 1. No palace write to CP6.
body.description:
Igor's 6 cornerposts (CP1-6) already largely follow approach-frame. CP1-CP5 are positive-framed. CP6 ('The world is not a safe place. We have to build and care for safety as we go.') is mixed: first sentence descriptive/negative, second positive.

**Task:** score each CP against approach-frame rule. Propose a reframe for CP6 (e.g. 'Build and care for safety continuously — the world requires it'). Surface proposal to Akien+Claude via T-audit-design-thread-pass2 BEFORE writing any palace node. HIGH-inertia (character sheets ~0.9x per project_character_sheets_are_living.md) — no write without Akien approval.

**Pre-approved:** Akien approved the audit itself and proposal-surface flow on 2026-04-21. Actual CP6 reframe write requires explicit Akien sign-off at sprint time.

**Affected files:** palace nodes at `theigors/character_sheets/cp*` (or equivalent — locate via `SELECT path FROM memory_palace WHERE path LIKE '%cp%' OR path LIKE '%character%'` at sprint start). No code files.

**Design rules:** no-sqlite (no persistence change); approach-frame (scoring against it); test-plan-or-why-not — see below.

**Scope boundary:**
- In: score CP1-6, produce scored table, propose CP6 reframe, surface for Akien approval.
- Out: other Igor memory rules beyond CP1-6 (T-igor-self-audit-approach-frame). Out: writing until approved.

**Gate:** T-palace-approach-frame-rule (need the scoring criterion).

**Test plan:** no new tests. Output = scored markdown table + CP6 reframe option presented to Akien. No palace write until explicitly approved.
